business.headline: Petrol and diesel prices likely to be hiked before May 15Petrol and diesel prices in India are likely to be hiked before May 15 as state-run oil marketing companies face rising financial stress amid a sharp surge in global crude oil prices, sources told India Today TV.

Bringing mothers in as co-applicants: Redefining home loan ownership in IndiaFamilies are now increasingly choosing to involve mothers in building long-term financial assets and securing a stronger financial future for themselves.

From flowers to financial freedom: Rethinking Mother's DayFor years, Mother's Day has been about flowers and celebrations. But slowly, conversations are shifting towards something deeper, i.e., helping mothers build savings, investments, and financial confidence of their own.

Explained: Why SBI shares tumbled nearly 7% after Q4 resultsSBI shares fell sharply after the lender's Q4 earnings disappointed the market. The reaction underscored investor concern over treasury income, margins and earnings quality.

Sensex closes over 500 points lower as SBI tumbles nearly 7% after Q4 resultsBenchmark indices closed sharply lower on Thursday after SBI's weaker-than-expected Q4 results hit banking shares. The decline reflected caution around crude oil prices, West Asia tensions and the broader earnings season.

Sensex sinks 600 points: Why is the stock market falling today?Benchmark indices fell sharply on Thursday as higher crude prices, foreign selling and weak banking stocks hit sentiment. Investors stayed cautious ahead of key Q4 results, with traders watching US-Iran tensions and oil moves.

I broke my FD 3 times: When it helped and when it hurtA fixed deposit feels safe because it is supposed to sit quietly in the background, growing slowly over time. But when life throws sudden surprises, that safety net often becomes the first thing people reach for. The real question is, when does breaking an FD actually make sense, and when does it backfire?

Think PAN is needed only for ITR Filing? 7 transactions where you must quote itPAN has quietly become one of the most important financial documents in India today, but are you aware that many banking, investment and cash transactions cannot be completed without quoting it?

8th Pay Commission: The family unit formula behind salary revisions of govt employeesEmployee unions have urged the 8th Pay Commission to revisit the family unit formula used to fix salaries. Any revision could raise basic pay, allowances, pensions and the broader compensation bill. But what exactly is the 'family unit' formula?

Tata Trusts board meeting postponed amid Tata Sons governance, listing discussionsTata Trusts has postponed a crucial board meeting scheduled for today to May 16, as governance disputes, Tata Sons board representation, listing discussions and leadership questions continue to draw attention across corporate India.

Lenskart shares fall over 2% amid block deal buzz, IPO lock-in expiryLenskart shares fell in Thursday's trade as block deal chatter coincided with the expiry of its IPO lock-in period. The move put the spotlight on possible supply pressure even as analysts said fundamentals remain unchanged.

Sensex opens 400 points lower, Nifty slips below 24,200 as banking stocks dragThe BSE Sensex fell 422.11 points, or 0.54%, to 77,422.41 in early trade. The NSE Nifty50 slipped 129 points to 24,197.65, falling below the 24,200 mark.

Why is Maharashtra's Rs 100 Will registration move getting so much attention?Anyone who owns assets such as a flat, land, jewellery, fixed deposits, shares or mutual funds should consider making a Will. Even small savings can become the reason for family disagreements if there is no clear-written instruction from the owner.

Pronto bags fresh $20 million funding, valuation jumps to $200 millionPronto offers quick home services such as laundry, kitchen preparation and home cleaning, with services starting from Rs 125. Workers are available within 15 minutes through the platform.

Stock market ends flat as Sensex slips 114, Nifty stays above 24,300Benchmark equity indices ended little changed on Wednesday, with the Sensex slipping 114 points and the Nifty holding above 24,300. The muted session underlined investor caution over global cues, interest rate expectations and earnings momentum, while selective buying capped losses.

Can senior citizens above skip ITR filing in FY27? Here's who qualifiesFiling income tax returns every year can be stressful for many elderly taxpayers. But did you know some senior citizens above 75 may not need to file ITR in FY27 at all if certain conditions are met?

Bought a car above Rs 10 lakh? You may be missing this tax creditCar buyers paying over Rs 10 lakh are being reminded to check the 1% TCS collected at purchase. The credit, linked to PAN records, can reduce tax dues or increase refunds at ITR filing.

Ex-CJI DY Chandrachud to mediate Sunjay Kapur family feud between mother, widowThe Supreme Court has pushed for a settlement in the Sunjay Kapur family trust dispute, with former CJI DY Chandrachud stepping in as mediator after both sides agreed to explore resolution through mediation.

DA hike explained: Why bank employees just got 0.70% while govt staff received 2%?A small DA hike for bank staff and a bigger one for government employees, has raised eyebrows. But is the difference unfair, or is there more to the calculation behind the scenes? Let us know here.

Explained: Why Paytm share price surged over 6% todayPaytm shares rose over 6% after One 97 Communications reported a Q4 profit and its first annual profit. The results signalled improving payments growth and margins even as regulatory concerns remained in focus.

entertainment.headline: Actor Genelia calls Vijay's election victory a win for Tamil Nadu Actor Genelia D'Souza said Thalapathy Vijay's election victory is a proud moment for Tamil Nadu. She said the result reflected public trust in him and faith that he would meet expectations.

Is Yash's global dream proving toxic to his pan-India superstardom? What began as anticipation has turned into a test of patience for fans. Yash's upcoming mega project Toxic promises to shape the actor's global ambition. But, given the multiple delays, is he getting too obsessed about delivering the perfect global product with Toxic?

Jackie Shroff's The Great Grand Superhero gets a release date: What to expect Jackie Shroff-starrer The Great Grand Superhero finally has a release date. The fantasy drama explores childhood imagination, aliens and the bond between a grandfather and his grandson.

Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge to get extended raw version on OTT. Details Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge will stream overseas from May 15. It will feature extended raw footage that was not part of the theatrical version.

Shekhar Suman says his debut film Utsav was ahead of its time, wants re-release Shekhar Suman revisited the films that shaped his career and said Utsav was ahead of its time. He also explained why he has now launched a film academy with a modern, experience-led approach to acting.

Prime Video, MX Player integrate: What it means for India's OTT landscape Amazon is integrating Prime Video and MX Player into a unified streaming ecosystem in India. The move brings free and paid viewing under one umbrella while widening its advertising and audience reach.

Rohit Sharma set to make his TV debut, teaser goes viral Indian cricketer Rohit Sharma teased his television debut with a promo built around his viral on-field remark. The clip has sparked excitement among fans, while details of the show's format are still awaited.

Daldalll: Jaideep Ahlawat trapped in a swamp in first poster of his new film Jaideep Ahlawat unveiled the first-look of his upcoming film Daldalll. The actor is shown trapped in a swamp in the gritty poster.

Alia Bhatt reviews Daadi Ki Shaadi, says Neetu Kapoor, Riddhima light up the screen Alia Bhatt shared her reaction after watching Daadi Ki Shaadi and said the film left her with a "warm smile." The actor especially praised Neetu Kapoor and debutante Riddhima Kapoor Sahni, saying they lit up the screen with their performances.

Su From So director JP Thuminad marries longtime girlfriend Rashmika Actor-director JP Thuminad, known for Su From So, married his longtime partner Rashmika in a traditional ceremony in Mangaluru on Tuesday. Wishes poured in online for the couple.

Urvashi Rautela on receiving Valentine's Day wish from Balakrishna: So cute Urvashi Rautela recalled the time when Nandamuri Balakrishna was the first person to wish her on Valentine's Day, adding that she was lucky to work with him in Daaku Maharaaj.

FIR against actor Celina Jaitly's husband over domestic violence allegations Celina Jaitly's legal battle with husband Peter Haag intensified after Mumbai Police initiated criminal proceedings against him based on her complaint. The actor had previously accused Haag of domestic violence, alleging she was denied access to her children in Austria.

Elderly man wins over internet with kind gesture towards Shahid Kapoor. Watch Shahid Kapoor's brief exchange with an elderly man at an airport queue has gone viral online. Social media users reacted to the man's gesture and the actor's courteous response.

The Devil Wears Prada returns after 20 years. Here's why it shouldn't have The Devil Wears Prada 2 banks on nostalgia but, unlike the first film, is no cultural landmark. The sequel touches upon several contemporary topics, though only superficially.

Legends review: Steve Coogan's thriller is the perfect blend of The Office, NarcosRelease Date:Thu May 07, 2026 Steve Coogan and Tom Burke lead Legends, a drama about customs officers sent undercover during Britain's heroin crisis. The series focuses on fear, exhaustion and the damage caused by living under false identities.

Remarkably Bright Creatures review: Warm, wistful and quietly movingRelease Date:Fri May 08, 2026 Netflix's Remarkably Bright Creatures adapts Shelby Van Pelt's novel into a gentler family drama led by Sally Field. The film keeps the grief and warmth intact but pushes its octopus protagonist to the margins.

29 review: Rathna Kumar's film has novel idea that loses its way before finding itRelease Date:Fri May 08, 2026 29 – The Film movie review: Director Rathna Kumar's 29, starring Vidhu and Preethi Asrani, is a romantic drama. The film is about a man who'll soon turn 30, and his tryst with love while battling an identity crisis. While the film's intentions are right, it takes unwanted detours before getting on the right path.

Mortal Kombat II review: Karl Urban's arcade bloodbath is dumb fun done rightRelease Date:Fri May 08, 2026 Mortal Kombat II The film brings Earthrealm into the long-delayed tournament against Shao Kahn's forces. Leaning fully into camp and carnage, Karl Urban's arcade bloodbath is dumb fun done right. Here is our review.

Citadel 2 review: Priyanka's show is smarter, slicker, slightly less exhaustingRelease Date:Wed May 06, 2026 Citadel Season 2 returns with cleaner momentum, sharper self-awareness and a more controlled spy spectacle. It remains excessive and emotionally thin, but the series is finally entertaining on its own terms.

general news.headline: Vijay is CM, or is he? Tamil Nadu's Game of ThronesTamil Nadu's post-poll drama swung wildly through Friday, leaving Vijay tantalisingly close to the CM post — yet still just short of certainty. At one point, he appeared set for a smooth swearing-in, only for last-minute gaps in support to pull the brakes.

TTV Dhinakaran files police complaint against Vijay's TVK over 'forged' MLA letterThe latest move followed TVK releasing a video of lone AMMK MLA S Kamaraj allegedly writing a support letter backing the Vijay-led party, directly countering Dhinakaran's earlier claim that a forged letter had been circulated.

Lieutenant General NS Raja Subramani named new Chief of Defence StaffLieutenant General NS Raja Subramani will replace General Anil Chauhan, who will be completing his tenure as CDS on May 30 this year. In another major military appointment, Vice Admiral Krishna Swaminathan has been appointed as the next Chief of the Indian Navy.

Live: Short of numbers Vijay unlikely to take oath today, Dhinakaran briefing at 11 amTamil Nadu Government Formation News Live Updates: Political uncertainty gripped Tamil Nadu as shifting alliance equations kept actor-politician Vijay on the edge of securing power. Initial optimism for the TVK grew after support signals from Congress and Left parties, but momentum weakened when the Indian Union Muslim League reaffirmed loyalty to the DMK-led alliance and the VCK delayed announcing its stand. Reports of intense negotiations exposed deep bargaining over cabinet representation, with VCK allegedly pressing for the Deputy Chief Minister's post for Thol Thirumavalavan, while TVK was unwilling to concede beyond a ministerial berth. Amid rumours of missing legislators and backchannel talks involving the AIADMK camp, Vijay met Governor Rajendra Vishwanath Arlekar to stake claim despite unresolved numbers. The crisis escalated further after Amma Makkal Munnetra Kazhagam filed a complaint accusing TVK of circulating a forged support letter linked to its lone MLA, intensifying mistrust and complicating coalition arithmetic. Stay tuned to IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

How Bengalis erected BJP fence after Jamaat's rise along Bangladesh borderThe Jamaat-e-Islami saw a surge in Bangladesh in the February election, particularly along the border with West Bengal. The rise of the Islamist party seems to have resulted in voter consolidation for the BJP across the border. The West Bengal Assembly election results show that the BJP won several seats in pockets directly adjacent to the seats won by the Jamaat.

Live: Suvendu Adhikari to take oath as Bengal's first BJP CM at 11 am, PM to attendSuvendu Adhikari Oath Ceremony Live Updates: West Bengal chief minister-elect Suvendu Adhikari on Friday met Governor RN Ravi and staked claim to form the state's first BJP-led government, hours after being elected leader of the legislature party in the presence of Union Home Minister Amit Shah. The BJP, which won 207 seats in the 294-member Assembly, will see Adhikari sworn in at Brigade Parade Grounds on Saturday in the presence of Prime Minister Narendra Modi, top Union ministers and chief ministers of BJP-ruled states.

Why Indian Army officers used English, asks Pak Army spox, gets trolled by PakistanisLt Gen Ahmed Sharif Chaudhry, the head of the Pakistani military's media wing, has questioned why Indian military officers were speaking in English while briefing on Operation Sindoor on its first anniversary. Pakistanis trolled Chaudhry, questioning English usage in Pakistani military documents instead of Urdu.

IPL Play of the Day: KKR's spin barrage turns Delhi's route away from playoffsIPL 2026, DC vs KKR: Kolkata Knight Riders' spinners choked Delhi Capitals through the middle overs after the home team got off to a brisk start at Arun Jaitley Stadium. On a retro night in Delhi, Sunil Narine, Anukul Roy and Varun Chakravarthy brought in the disco twist to keep KKR alive in the play-off race.

Is Yash's global dream proving toxic to his pan-India superstardom?What began as anticipation has turned into a test of patience for fans. Yash's upcoming mega project Toxic promises to shape the actor's global ambition. But, given the multiple delays, is he getting too obsessed about delivering the perfect global product with Toxic?

Suvendu Adhikari's rise: From Nandigram giant killer to Bengal Chief MinisterOnce Mamata Banerjee's trusted lieutenant, Suvendu Adhikari is now set to take oath as West Bengal Chief Minister today after scripting BJP's biggest breakthrough and defeating his former mentor once again.

Confusion over Vijay's oath as TVK submits only 116 signatures to GovernorTamil Nadu's post-poll drama took another twist as TVK chief Vijay appeared set to be sworn in as Chief Minister on Saturday, but the ceremony was delayed after Governor Rajendra Arlekar awaited a formal support letter from DMK ally, VCK, leaving TVK two short of the majority mark with 116 MLAs.

Bhoy out, bhorsha in: Suvendu Adhikari's message after being picked as Bengal CMWith Union Home Minister Amit Shah announcing Suvendu Adhikari as West Bengal's next chief minister, the BJP is set to form its first-ever government in the state after a historic electoral sweep.

Big disclosure: Chinese personnel gave on-site support to Pak during Op SindoorTwo Chinese engineers have revealed that they provided on-site technical support to Pakistan during Operation Sindoor. The disclosure from the engineers, who were present in Pakistan during the May 2025 Operation Sindoor, is the first official acceptance that Chinese personnel were involved in the four-day mini-war with India.

DMK backed outside support to AIADMK govt, asked us to follow: CPM's big claimIn a major revelation, CPI(M) leader MA Baby said the DMK had floated the idea of extending outside support to an AIADMK government and urged the Left to follow suit. He said his party rejected the proposal and instead backed Vijay's TVK to "defend the people's verdict".

Where did summer go? Unusual cool May surprises India, heat dipsOn May 7, a region in Uttar Pradesh recorded a maximum temperature of just 26°C, making it the coolest May day ever measured there. What's happening?

OpenAI, Anthropic starting service companies is good news for Indian IT sectorAnthropic and OpenAI are entering the world of SaaS – software as a service – but with AI. This may come across as bad news for Indian IT giants like TCS and Infosys. But look closer, it is actually very good news for them and for Indian IT employees.

Hantavirus explained: Early symptoms are mild, but organ failure follows fastA hantavirus outbreak on a luxury cruise ship has killed three passengers and sparked concern among health experts. While the virus can be severe and even life-threatening in some cases, doctors say it spreads very differently from highly contagious respiratory infections like Covid-19.

Question Time: Weekly News QuizQuestion Time invites readers to test how closely they followed the week's news. The quiz turns the biggest developments into a quick check of current affairs awareness.

Chidambaram admits Congress underestimated Vijay after Tamil Nadu's TVK shockerP Chidambaram said Congress and the wider political establishment had underestimated Vijay and TVK before the Tamil Nadu verdict. He said Congress backed TVK after the results to avoid another election and President's Rule.

Sam Altman removed as OpenAI CEO, now fresh texts with Mira Murati reveal dramaText messages shared between Mira Murati and Sam Altman have made a sudden appearance in the OpenAI versus Elon Musk trial spilling the beans about the time of turmoil when Altman was fired from OpenAI. Here are the details.

TMC lost Bengal and the Muslim vote. But who gained from it?Two things happened in the 2026 West Bengal polls: the BJP's consolidation of Hindu votes and the end of the Trinamool Congress's absolute dominance over Muslim voters.

Russia, Ukraine agree to 3-day Trump-mediated ceasefire, prisoner swapRussia and Ukraine confirmed a US-mediated three-day ceasefire and a 1,000-for-1,000 prisoner swap. The deal covered the Victory Day period even as both sides reported fresh overnight attacks.

WHO confirms 6 Hantavirus cases, 3 deaths linked to Spain-bound cruise shipThe WHO has confirmed a hantavirus outbreak linked to a cruise ship after eight people fell ill and three died. The agency said the wider global risk remains low, though the onboard risk is moderate.

Rain on its way: Signal from Australia hints monsoon on track to IndiaThunderstorms are crackling over Tamil Nadu and Kerala, with heavy rain warnings in place across south India. When is monsoon coming?

Actor Genelia calls Vijay's election victory a win for Tamil NaduActor Genelia D'Souza said Thalapathy Vijay's election victory is a proud moment for Tamil Nadu. She said the result reflected public trust in him and faith that he would meet expectations.

Is Yash's global dream proving toxic to his pan-India superstardom?What began as anticipation has turned into a test of patience for fans. Yash's upcoming mega project Toxic promises to shape the actor's global ambition. But, given the multiple delays, is he getting too obsessed about delivering the perfect global product with Toxic?

sports.headline: New RR owner Laxmi Mittal meets team ahead of GT tie, chats with Riyan, Jaiswal New Rajasthan Royals owner Lakshmi Mittal visited the squad in Jaipur and spoke at length with Riyan Parag and Yashasvi Jaiswal. The appearance came as the franchise chases a play-off place while a multibillion-dollar ownership change moves towards completion.

IPL Play of the Day: KKR's spin barrage turns Delhi's route away from playoffs IPL 2026, DC vs KKR: Kolkata Knight Riders' spinners choked Delhi Capitals through the middle overs after the home team got off to a brisk start at Arun Jaitley Stadium. On a retro night in Delhi, Sunil Narine, Anukul Roy and Varun Chakravarthy brought in the disco twist to keep KKR alive in the play-off race.

How KKR can qualify for playoffs after incredible comeback: Qualification Scenario IPL 2026, DC vs KKR: Ajinkya Rahane's Kolkata Knight Riders have more than a realistic chance to qualify for the playoffs after they beat the Delhi Capitals by 8 wickets on Friday in New Delhi.

The Jason Holder effect: Gujarat Titans enjoying all-rounder's IPL 2026 surge IPL 2026: Parthiv Patel said Jason Holder's inclusion has changed Gujarat Titans' balance ahead of the Rajasthan Royals match. His all-round returns have stabilised the middle order and strengthened the bowling options.

No Instagram following, no LPL: Sri Lanka League sets odd criteria for players Lanka Premier League has set odd criteria for categorising its players. If a player wants to be in the league as a top tier Icon category, they need to either play 75 T20Is or have, wait for it, 250,000 social media followers.

Novak Djokovic knocked out of Italian Open by his mirror image Novak Djokovic suffered a shock early exit at the Italian Open after Croatian youngster Dino Prizmic fought back from a set down to beat the Serbian 2-6, 6-2, 6-4 in Rome. The defeat has raised fresh concerns over his fitness and form ahead of the French Open later this month.

No deal between India and Russia in athletics: AFI snubs rumours The Athletics Federation of India has denied reports claiming that it signed a cooperation agreement with Russian Athletics to allow Russian athletes to compete in India. AFI spokesperson Adille Sumariwalla said India would continue to follow World Athletics sanctions imposed on Russia.

Finn-ishing blow: KKR back in playoffs race as DC's home misery continues IPL 2026, DC vs KKR: A Finn Allen century and a spin-bowling masterclass saw Kolkata Knight Riders crush Delhi Capitals at the Kotla. The clinical win revives KKR's playoff hopes while leaving Delhi's campaign in tatters.

Season finished: Delhi Capitals to start planning for next year, says Axar Patel Delhi Capitals captain Axar Patel admitted that his side has effectively fallen out of the IPL 2026 playoffs race after their defeat to Kolkata Knight Riders. Axar said Delhi will now start planning for next season and test their bench strength.

IPL 2026: Angkrish Raghuvanshi's streak of bizarre dismissals continues IPL 2026: Angkrish Raghuvanshi suffered another bizarre dismissal during Kolkata Knight Riders' chase against Delhi Capitals. The freak wicket added to a season already marked by unusual exits, though this one brought laughter rather than controversy.

Stop treating CWG, Asian Games like paid holidays: Govt warns administrators Indian sports administrators have been warned to not treat events like Commonwealth and Asian Games like paid vacations. Sources say that if found lackadaisical, they will be sent back home midway through the tournament.

Madrid fine Valverde, Tchouameni Rs 5.5 core each after mega brawl in trainingReal Madrid have fined Federico Valverde and Aurelien Tchouameni Rs 5.5 crore each after the pair were involved in a heated training-ground altercation ahead of El Clasico. The club confirmed that both players apologised to each other, the squad and the fans before accepting the punishment.

Gautam Gambhir seen at DC vs KKR, shares long chat with India selectorIPL 2026: India head coach Gautam Gambhir attended the Delhi Capitals-Kolkata Knight Riders match and spent long periods with selector R P Singh. Their presence together at Arun Jaitley Stadium stirred talk of scouting and selection ahead of a busy international schedule.

Bournemouth player dropped after club probes alleged chats with minorAFC Bournemouth have left Alex Jimenez out of their squad after online posts prompted an internal investigation. The development comes at a key stage in the club's push for European qualification.

Aryna Sabalenka to remain neutral athlete as ITF rejects IOC's Belarus policy stanceThe ITF has confirmed that Russian and Belarusian players, including Aryna Sabalenka, will continue competing as neutral athletes despite the IOC's recommendation to ease restrictions on Belarusian competitors.

Hopeful Guardiola wants Manchester City to keep pressure on Arsenal in title raceManchester City manager Pep Guardiola has urged his side to keep the pressure firmly on Arsenal in the Premier League title race, insisting City can only focus on winning their remaining matches as the season heads into its decisive stretch.

Iga Swiatek escapes major Italian Open upset, but French Open worries persistIga Swiatek survived a stern challenge from Caty McNally at the Italian Open, recovering from a second-set wobble to seal a hard-fought three-set win and advance to the next round in Rome.

Built on blue-collar roots, Sean Brady eyeing run for UFC Welterweight goldBlue-collar roots, fatherhood and trust in his process keeps Sean Brady steady in his UFC career. Speaking ahead of his bout with Joaquin Buckley, Brady talks about his title aspirations, how he loves keeping away from social media for hype and more.

England receive huge Test boost as Stokes makes instant bowling impact on returnEngland received a timely boost ahead of the home Test summer after captain Ben Stokes made an immediate impact with the ball on his competitive return for Durham in the County Championship.

Najmul Shanto grinds Pakistan down as Bangladesh seize edge in Mirpur TestBAN vs PAK: Najmul Hossain Shanto struck a superb century while Mominul Haque narrowly missed out on a hundred as Bangladesh recovered from an early wobble to finish Day 1 of the Mirpur Test strongly at 301 for four against Pakistan.

Hey Gambhir, are you watching? Prince's push for India debut can't be ignoredPrince Yadav shines in IPL 2026 with a stunning spell vs RCB. With a sensational skill set and brilliant mentality, Prince continues his push for getting a call-up into the Indian team.

India U17 women keep historic Asian Cup quarterfinal and World Cup dream aliveIndia's U17 women beat Lebanon 4-0 in Suzhou to stay in the AFC U17 Women's Asian Cup quarterfinal race. The result lifted their goal difference to -1 and kept their FIFA U17 Women's World Cup hopes alive.

Super Rapid and Blitz: Gukesh beats Caruana in final round of Rapid eventWorld champion D Gukesh defeated American Grandmaster Fabiano Caruana in the final round of the rapid section to secure a tied-fourth finish at the Super Rapid and Blitz Tournament.

MS Dhoni named as highest individual taxpayer in Bihar-Jharkhand regionMS Dhoni has again been named the highest individual taxpayer in the Bihar-Jharkhand region for 2025-26. The announcement underlines his enduring commercial pull even as questions over his IPL future continue.

Gavaskar calls for major cricket rule tweak, urges Ganguly to help pacersFormer India captain Sunil Gavaskar urged former India skipper Sourav Ganguly, who currently chairs the ICC Men's Cricket Committee, to consider changes to the wide bouncer rule to give fast bowlers more leeway in modern-day cricket.

Real Madrid chaos: What we know so far as Tchouameni sends Valverde to hospitalReal Madrid open disciplinary action after Tchouameni-Valverde clash. Here's how the dressing-room bust-up unfolded and what happens next.

Watch: After dismissing Kohli for duck, Prince shares special moment with RCB iconLSG vs RCB: Virat Kohli was seen chatting with Lucknow Super Giants pacer Prince Yadav and bowling coach Bharat Arun following LSG's win over Royal Challengers Bengaluru in IPL 2026 at the Ekana Stadium.

Surya likely to lose India T20I captaincy, Shreyas frontrunner to take overBCCI are likely to discuss India's T20I captaincy in the upcoming selection meeting, with Suryakumar Yadav's place under threat and Shreyas Iyer emerging as the frontrunner to take over.

Not money or parades, just want to know India is watching us: SatwikSatwiksairaj Rankireddy has clarified that his remarks after India's Thomas Cup bronze were about recognition, not personal fame. He said silent receptions for such achievements can discourage athletes and called for equal support across sports.

Rajat Patidar steers clear of defensive mindset: Attack, be champions againLSG vs RCB: Rajat Patidar says RCB are not approaching IPL 2026 as defending champions, insisting the team will keep an attacking mindset as they push for another title despite a recent slump.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
